claim_text: Cortical inhibition timing can stabilize recurrent network activity under fluctuating inputs.
normalized_claim: cortical inhibition timing stabilize recurrent network activity fluctuating input

citation_contexts.supporting_passage: Optogenetic activation of fast-spiking interneurons increased temporal precision and reduced runaway excitation.
citation_contexts.notes: Direct physiological support in mouse V1.

citation_contexts.supporting_passage: Inhibitory activity scaled with excitatory drive and constrained gain.
citation_contexts.notes: Supports stabilization but in narrower task context.

traceability.rationale: All references resolve and include supporting passages.
evidence: 2 citation contexts with DOI and passage source
traceability.recommended_fix: Require DOI lookup and passage extraction before acceptance.

robustness.rationale: Convergent support from two studies using related methods.
robustness.recommended_fix: Add explicit replication evidence across labs.

uncertainty_calibration.recommended_fix: Use suggestive/uncertain language when replication is sparse.

source_integrity.rationale: Primary sources with DOI verification and full-text retrieval for key support.
source_integrity.recommended_fix: Drop retracted sources and require at least abstract-level traceability.

transferability_scope_control.rationale: Evidence is mostly rodent V1; broader cortical transfer remains uncertain.
transferability_scope_control.recommended_fix: Constrain claim scope explicitly to tested system.
